title: Dominican Republic Visa Application Form (Formulario Solicitud de Visa)
description: Application filed by a foreign national at a Dominican consular office abroad to request a Dominican Republic entry visa, using the Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores' (MIREX) 2-page 'Formulario Solicitud de Visa', served from the Portal de Servicios Consulares (servicios360.mirex.gob.do). The form is print-and-fill (no AcroForm layer; both pages are native vector-drawn tables with a genuine embedded text layer, not scanned images) and is freely downloadable with no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate; the same host also serves a byte-for-byte English-language edition used in this document as a cross-check on field labels and structure. It models the applicant's biographic data (surnames, names, birth/nationality), current residence and contact details, passport and optional secondary identity-document data, marital status and (where applicable) spouse data, the trip's purpose/dates/duration, planned Dominican accommodation, current employer/place-of-study data, who (if not the applicant) is funding the trip, the applicant's Dominican visa/entry history (prior visits, prior grants, cancellations/revocations, denials), work/study intent while in the Dominican Republic, whether anyone else has petitioned a visa on the applicant's behalf, accompanying travelers, and the supporting-document checklist the applicant brings to substantiate the request. Unlike do/mirex/passport-application (which carries the source's own '* Campos obligatorios' asterisk convention), this form prints no required-field marker of any kind on either page; requiredness in this version is asserted from context — the form's own explicit conditional-answer instructions ('si es afirmativo', 'si es usted mismo, no llenar') and this registry's established default of treating a field as required unless the source itself discloses it as optional or purely supplementary (see uy/mrree/formulario-unificado-de-visas, judgment call 4, for the precedent this document follows) — not from any printed convention. Two categories of the source form are out of scope: the 'Información Interna (No llenar)' office box (Expediente/Resolución number, date, place, status — explicitly marked 'do not fill in' on the form itself, consular-office tracking metadata rather than applicant-supplied data); and the 'Firma Solicitante'/'Dedo 1 (Índice Izquierdo)'/'Dedo 2 (Índice Derecho)' block (wet-ink signature plus two fingerprint captures), excluded as physical biometric/signature capture performed in the presence of consular staff, consistent with this registry's treatment of biometric capture elsewhere (e.g. do/mirex/passport-application, mx/sre/passport-application).

verification.notes: Source re-fetched fresh this cycle (not trusted from the GOV-3152 scouting note as-is): HTTP 200, media type application/pdf, 100,161 bytes, sha256 398cb3629e1844f3064b62e3943563314c3121d50bcfa56ab974055abe0715a4, genuine %PDF-1.3-family header, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate. The disclosed English-language sibling (https://servicios360.mirex.gob.do/pdf/formularios/Formulario_Solicitud_Visado_english.pdf, 239,212 bytes, sha256 347913db33ac524baf6ef0434c9dacf37ded194e3a0bb7deef53293be697fd97) was also re-fetched fresh and used only as a cross-check on field labels/section structure, not as the canonical source. pdfjs-dist 3.11.174 confirmed 2 pages and page.getAnnotations() returning 0 for both pages of both editions — a print-and-fill form with no AcroForm layer. Unlike do/mirex/passport-application, a getTextContent() extraction pass recovered every field label on both pages directly (0 items containing '*' on either page — confirmed there is no asterisk/required-marker convention anywhere on this form, in either language edition), so no text-extraction gap was encountered here; nonetheless every section was independently cross-checked against a node-canvas render of both pages at 6x scale (Section 4.1 'Sexo' and Section 8.1 'Estado Civil' were specifically re-examined at this resolution and confirmed to be blank boxes with no printed checkbox options, unlike the same-named fields on do/mirex/passport-application), and every repeating table's exact row count (Section 11: 3 rows; Section 13: 4 rows; Sections 14/15/17/19: 2 rows each; Section 18: 2 lettered blocks a/b; Section 20: 3 rows) was counted directly off the render, not assumed. Field count: 136 fields plus 1 documents[] entry (36-item supporting-document checklist).

fields.name: secondaryNationality
fields.label: Segunda Nacionalidad
fields.description: Only completed if the applicant holds a second nationality; the English-language variant of this form renders this label as 'Secondary nationality (if applicable)', confirming the qualifier the Spanish original leaves implicit.

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sexo
fields.description: Unlike do/mirex/passport-application's 'Sexo' field (which reproduces the passport form's own printed Femenino/Masculino checkbox options), this visa form's Section 4.1 is a single blank box with no printed options of any kind — confirmed by a 6x node-canvas render of the surrounding table. Modeled as free text rather than fabricating an enum the source does not print.

fields.label: Mayor grado académico alcanzado
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 4.2. Mayor grado académico alcanzado

fields.label: País donde se emitió el pasaporte

fields.label: Localidad donde se emitió el pasaporte

fields.label: País al que pertenece el pasaporte

fields.name: secondaryIdDocumentCountry
fields.label: País del Documento
fields.description: Section 7 is a single-row grid for an additional personal identity document beyond the passport (e.g. a national ID card); nothing on the form indicates every applicant holds one, so the whole section is modeled as optional supplementary data.

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Estado Civil
fields.description: No checkbox options are printed for this field (unlike do/mirex/passport-application's Estado Civil, which reproduces five printed options) — modeled as free text.

fields.name: spouseFullName
fields.label: Nombre completo de su Esposo/a (aunque esté divorciado/a)
fields.description: Because maritalStatus (8.1) is modeled as free text rather than an enum (no printed options), there is no reliable value to key a requiredWhen condition against for the spouse block — modeled as optional throughout, consistent with this registry's established treatment of the same situation (see uy/mrree/formulario-unificado-de-visas judgment call 4).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 8.2. Nombre completo de su Esposo/a (aunque esté divorciado/a)

fields.label: Fecha Nacimiento del Esposo/a

fields.label: ¿En qué fecha planea viajar?

fields.label: ¿Por qué motivo?
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 9.3. ¿Por qué motivo? — Placer / Estudio / Negocio / Trabajo / Dependencia / Oficial / Diplomático / Residencia / Cortesía / Otros

fields.label: Nombre (hotel o persona física)

fields.label: Nombre de la Vía (alojamiento)
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 10.8. Nombre de la Vía

fields.name: accommodationStreetNumber
fields.label: Número (alojamiento)
fields.description: The source PDF prints the label '10. 8 Número' for this box — a duplicate of the immediately preceding 'Nombre de la Vía' field's own '10.8' label (a genuine numbering artifact in the form itself, confirmed in both the Spanish and English editions), not a re-entry of the street name field. Modeled as its own field per its distinct 'Número' caption and grid position.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 10.8 [sic, duplicate source numbering]. Número

fields.name: employer1Activity
fields.label: Actividad (empleador/centro de estudio 1)
fields.description: Section 11's own heading qualifies repeated rows with 'anotar más de uno si existen' (note more than one if they exist); the whole 3-row block is modeled as optional, including its first row, since nothing on the form indicates every applicant currently has an employer or place of study.

fields.name: travelExpensesPayerName
fields.label: Nombre (quién pagará los gastos)
fields.description: The source's own parenthetical instruction ('Si es usted mismo, no llenar' — if it is you yourself, do not fill in) makes this section conditional on a third party paying; modeled as optional since the form provides no boolean gate field to key a requiredWhen condition on.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, Section 12.1. Nombre (Si es usted mismo, no llenar)

fields.name: visitedDominicanRepublicBefore
fields.label: ¿Ha visitado República Dominicana alguna vez?
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section 13. ¿Ha visitado República Dominicana alguna vez? ___ Si ___ No

fields.name: previousVisit1Date
fields.label: ¿En qué fecha? (visita previa 1)
fields.description: The source instructs answering 'empezando por la visita más recientes' (starting with the most recent visit); this most-recent-visit row is required once visitedDominicanRepublicBefore is true, while rows 2-4 are additional entries modeled as optional, consistent with this registry's entrantN convention (e.g. dk/cpr/notification-of-entry).

fields.name: previouslyGrantedDominicanVisa

fields.name: priorVisaGranted1Date
fields.label: Fecha (visa DR otorgada 1)
fields.description: Note: Colocar aquí sólo los datos para los cuales aporta constancia al momento de presentar esta solicitud (only include information provable at the time of filing) qualifies this whole sub-block; required once previouslyGrantedDominicanVisa is true, row 2 modeled as an optional additional entry.
